import { Command, CommandContext, CommandHandler } from '../types';
import { CommandError } from '../errors';
import { CommandRegistry } from './registry';

/**
 * Базовый класс для команд
 */
export abstract class BaseCommand implements Command {
  readonly name: string;
  readonly description: string;
  readonly usage: string;
  readonly aliases: string[];
  readonly cooldown: number;
  readonly permissions: string[];
  readonly botPermissions: string[];
  readonly guildOnly: boolean;
  readonly nsfw: boolean;
  readonly enabled: boolean;

  constructor(options: Partial<Command> & { name: string }) {
    this.name = options.name;
    this.description = options.description || '';
    this.usage = options.usage || '';
    this.aliases = options.aliases || [];
    this.cooldown = options.cooldown || 0;
    this.permissions = options.permissions || [];
    this.botPermissions = options.botPermissions || [];
    this.guildOnly = options.guildOnly || false;
    this.nsfw = options.nsfw || false;
    this.enabled = options.enabled ?? true;
  }

  abstract execute(context: CommandContext): Promise<void>;
}

/**
 * Декоратор для регистрации команды
 */
export function registerCommand(command: Command): void {
  CommandRegistry.register(command);
}

/**
 * Декоратор для установки кулдауна команды
 */
export function cooldown(seconds: number): MethodDecorator {
  return (target: any, propertyKey: string | symbol, descriptor: PropertyDescriptor) => {
    const originalMethod = descriptor.value;
    
    descriptor.value = async function(...args: any[]) {
      const context = args[0] as CommandContext;
      const commandName = typeof propertyKey === 'string' ? propertyKey : 'unknown';
      
      // Проверка кулдауна будет реализована в CommandHandler
      return originalMethod.apply(this, args);
    };
    
    return descriptor;
  };
}

/**
 * Декоратор для установки необходимых разрешений
 */
export function requiredPermissions(...permissions: string[]): MethodDecorator {
  return (target: any, propertyKey: string | symbol, descriptor: PropertyDescriptor) => {
    const originalMethod = descriptor.value;
    
    descriptor.value = async function(...args: any[]) {
      const context = args[0] as CommandContext;
      
      // Проверка разрешений будет реализована в CommandHandler
      return originalMethod.apply(this, args);
    };
    
    return descriptor;
  };
}

/**
 * Декоратор для указания, что команда только для серверов
 */
export function guildOnly(): MethodDecorator {
  return (target: any, propertyKey: string | symbol, descriptor: PropertyDescriptor) => {
    const originalMethod = descriptor.value;
    
    descriptor.value = async function(...args: any[]) {
      const context = args[0] as CommandContext;
      
      if (!context.guild) {
        throw new CommandError('Эта команда может использоваться только на сервере');
      }
      
      return originalMethod.apply(this, args);
    };
    
    return descriptor;
  };
}

/**
 * Декоратор для указания, что команда только для NSFW каналов
 */
export function nsfw(): MethodDecorator {
  return (target: any, propertyKey: string | symbol, descriptor: PropertyDescriptor) => {
    const originalMethod = descriptor.value;
    
    descriptor.value = async function(...args: any[]) {
      const context = args[0] as CommandContext;
      
      if (!context.channel || !('nsfw' in context.channel) || !(context.channel as any).nsfw) {
        throw new CommandError('Эта команда может использоваться только в NSFW каналах');
      }
      
      return originalMethod.apply(this, args);
    };
    
    return descriptor;
  };
}

# 🌐 Multilingual Text Classification

This repository contains the code and resources for the paper **"Multilingual Text Classification: A Comprehensive Survey"**.

## 📄 Paper

You can access the paper here: [Multilingual Text Classification: A Comprehensive Survey](https://arxiv.org/abs/2409.13355)

## 📚 Abstract

Text classification is a fundamental task in natural language processing (NLP) that involves assigning predefined categories to text data. With the increasing availability of multilingual data, multilingual text classification has become a critical area of research. This survey provides a comprehensive overview of the current state of multilingual text classification, covering key aspects such as datasets, models, techniques, and evaluation metrics. We discuss the challenges and opportunities in this field, including language diversity, resource scarcity, and cross-lingual transfer learning. Additionally, we highlight recent advancements and future directions, aiming to inspire further research and development in multilingual text classification.

## 📂 Repository Structure

- `data/`: Contains datasets used in the study.
- `models/`: Includes pre-trained models and model architectures.
- `scripts/`: Contains scripts for data preprocessing, training, and evaluation.
- `results/`: Stores the results and outputs from experiments.
- `notebooks/`: Jupyter notebooks for exploratory data analysis and experiments.

## 🚀 Getting Started

To get started with the code in this repository, follow these steps:

1. Clone the repository:
    ```bash
    git clone https://github.com/yourusername/multilingual-text-classification.git
    ```
2. Navigate to the repository directory:
    ```bash
    cd multilingual-text-classification
    ```
3. Install the required dependencies:
    ```bash
    pip install -r requirements.txt
    ```
4. Run the scripts or notebooks as needed.

## 📊 Datasets

The datasets used in this study are available in the `data/` directory. Each dataset is accompanied by a README file with detailed descriptions and usage instructions.

## 🤖 Models

Pre-trained models and model architectures are available in the `models/` directory. Instructions for using these models are provided in the respective README files.

## 📈 Results

Experimental results and outputs are stored in the `results/` directory. Each result file includes a brief description and interpretation of the findings.

## 📝 Notebooks

Jupyter notebooks for exploratory data analysis and experiments are available in the `notebooks/` directory. These notebooks provide step-by-step guides and visualizations.

## 🤝 Contributing

We welcome contributions to this repository. If you have any suggestions, improvements, or bug fixes, please submit a pull request or open an issue.

## 📧 Contact

For any questions or inquiries, please contact us at [your-email@example.com].

---

Thank you for your interest in our work on multilingual text classification!

# 📂 Data Directory

This directory contains the datasets used for the **Multilingual Text Classification** survey and experiments.

## 📁 Contents

| Dataset | Description | Languages | Task Type | Source |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| `amazon_reviews_multi` | Amazon product reviews with star ratings. | 10+ (en, de, fr, es, ja, zh, etc.) | Sentiment Analysis | Amazon |
| `mlsum` | Large-scale summarization dataset (often adapted for classification). | 5 (de, es, fr, ru, tr) | Summarization/Classification | News APIs |
| `xnli` | Cross-lingual Natural Language Inference. | 15 | NLI / Classification | Translated MNLI |
| `masakhanews` | News headlines and articles. | 10 African languages | Topic Classification | Online News |
| `indic_nlp` | Collection of datasets for Indian languages. | 11+ Indian languages | Various | Various |
| `wikiann` | Wikipedia-based Named Entity Recognition (often used for text classification tasks). | 176+ | NER / Classification | Wikipedia |

*(Note: Due to copyright and licensing restrictions, raw data files are not always included directly. Scripts to download and preprocess these datasets are provided below.)*

## 🛠️ Usage

### 1. Automatic Download & Preprocessing
We recommend using the provided Python script to download and prepare the datasets in the correct format.

```bash
python scripts/download_data.py --dataset amazon_reviews_multi --lang all
python scripts/preprocess.py --input data/raw --output data/processed
```

# 🌐 Multilingual Text Classification

This repository contains the code and resources for the paper titled **"Multilingual Text Classification: A Comprehensive Survey"**. The paper provides an in-depth review of multilingual text classification, covering datasets, models, evaluation metrics, and future research directions.

## 📄 Paper

You can access the paper on arXiv: [Multilingual Text Classification: A Comprehensive Survey](https://arxiv.org/abs/2409.13355)

## 📚 Abstract

Text classification is a fundamental task in natural language processing (NLP) that involves assigning predefined categories to text data. With the increasing availability of multilingual data, there is a growing need for models that can effectively handle multiple languages. This survey provides a comprehensive overview of multilingual text classification, covering key aspects such as datasets, models, evaluation metrics, and future research directions. We discuss the challenges associated with multilingual text classification, including language diversity, data scarcity, and cultural nuances. Additionally, we review state-of-the-art models and techniques, highlighting their strengths and limitations. Finally, we outline potential areas for future research, emphasizing the importance of developing more robust and adaptable multilingual text classification systems.

## 📂 Repository Structure

- `data/`: Contains datasets used for multilingual text classification.
- `models/`: Includes code for various multilingual models.
- `evaluation/`: Scripts for evaluating model performance.
- `notebooks/`: Jupyter notebooks with examples and experiments.
- `README.md`: This file, providing an overview of the repository.

## 🚀 Getting Started

To get started with the code and experiments, follow these steps:

1. Clone the repository:
    ```bash
    git clone https://github.com/yourusername/multilingual-text-classification.git
    ```
2. Navigate to the repository:
    ```bash
    cd multilingual-text-classification
    ```
3. Install the required dependencies:
    ```bash
    pip install -r requirements.txt
    ```
4. Explore the notebooks and scripts to understand the implementation details.

## 📊 Datasets

We provide links to several multilingual datasets commonly used in text classification tasks. These datasets cover a wide range of languages and domains.

## 🤖 Models

The repository includes implementations of various multilingual models, including:
- mBERT (Multilingual BERT)
- XLM-R (Cross-lingual Language Model)
- mT5 (Multilingual T5)

## 📈 Evaluation

Scripts for evaluating model performance on different datasets are provided. These scripts compute standard metrics such as accuracy, F1-score, and more.

## 🔮 Future Work

We encourage researchers to explore the following areas for future work:
- Developing models that can handle low-resource languages.
- Improving model robustness to cultural and contextual nuances.
- Exploring new evaluation metrics tailored for multilingual tasks.

## 📬 Contact

For any questions or suggestions, please open an issue or contact the authors directly.

## 📜 License

This project is licensed under the MIT License.

---

We hope this repository serves as a valuable resource for researchers and practitioners working on multilingual text classification. Happy coding! 🌍📚
